hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The matching requires a strict equality check on the 'asset_identifier'. If the event asset does not match the predicate asset exactly (including case and casing nuances), the event does not match, even if the action is accepted.

No transactions are ever selected by this form of the predicate when the scope is 'contract_deployment' with 'implement_trait'. The result will always be triggered=false, count=0, and an empty list, regardless of the blocks analyzed.

context_pointers.vague_reference: utilize the default sorting key from Block.java
context_pointers.actual_meaning: transactions within a block must be sorted first by their block_index (ascending integer), then by their global position within that specific block (ascending relative index), effectively creating a block aware global sort.

context_pointers.vague_reference: handle the catch-all config for contract lookup
context_pointers.actual_meaning: the predicate must either contain the precise 'contract_identifier' string defined in the protocol, or explicitly contain the literal string '*' to match any identifier.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_ft_event.json.description: Evaluate a fungible-token event predicate against a sequence of blocks. The predicate names a token asset identifier and a set of accepted actions (mint, transfer, burn). A block is selected when it holds a transaction that emitted a fungible-token event whose action is among the accepted set AND whose asset identifier equals the one named by the predicate. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

feature2_nft_event.json.description: Evaluate a non-fungible-token event predicate against a sequence of blocks. The predicate names a token asset identifier and a set of accepted actions (mint, transfer, burn). A block is selected when it holds a transaction that emitted a non-fungible-token event whose action is among the accepted set AND whose asset identifier equals the one named by the predicate. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

feature3_stx_event.json.description: Evaluate a native-token event predicate against a sequence of blocks. The predicate names a set of accepted actions (mint, transfer, lock, burn) and does not constrain any asset identifier. A block is selected when it holds a transaction that emitted a native-token event whose action is among the accepted set. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

feature4_print_event.json.description: Evaluate a smart-contract print-event predicate against a sequence of blocks. The predicate names a target contract identifier and a substring constraint. Only events published under the print topic are considered. A print event matches when its publishing contract identifier equals the predicate's contract identifier (or the predicate uses the wildcard '*' for the contract identifier) AND the human-readable decoding of the event's payload contains the predicate's substring (or the predicate uses the wildcard '*' for the substring, which matches any payload). A block is selected when it holds a transaction emitting at least one matching print event. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

feature5_contract_deployment.json.description: Evaluate a contract-deployment predicate against a sequence of blocks that may contain both contract-deployment and contract-call transactions. Two predicate forms exist. In the deployer form, a deployment transaction is selected when its deployed contract identifier begins with the named deployer prefix, and the wildcard '*' selects every deployment transaction. In the implemented-trait form (selecting deployments that implement a standard token trait), no transaction is selected because trait detection is not performed. Only deployment transactions can ever be selected by this predicate. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

feature6_contract_call.json.description: Evaluate a contract-call predicate against a sequence of blocks that may contain both contract-call and contract-deployment transactions. The predicate names a target contract identifier and a target method name. A contract-call transaction is selected when both its called contract identifier and its invoked method name equal the values named by the predicate. Only contract-call transactions can be selected. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

feature7_txid.json.description: Evaluate a transaction-identifier predicate against a sequence of blocks. The predicate names an exact transaction identifier hash. A transaction is selected when its identifier hash equals the named value, regardless of the transaction's kind. The report states whether any block was selected, how many blocks were selected, and the identifiers of the matched transactions.

_constraints.output_rule: The adapter must output exactly three lines to stdout in the order specified, followed by a newline.

When no transactions match, the third line must be 'matched_transactions='
empty line, followed by a newline.
A block is counted as selected (incrementing apply_block_count) if at least one of its transactions matches the predicate.
Only blocks that have at least one matching transaction contribute their matched transactions to the final csv string. Transactions from non-selected blocks are ignored.
The matched_transactions csv must contain txids ordered first by block index (ascending), then by transaction index within that block (ascending).

A transaction matches if it has events where the event type is 'FTMintEvent', 'FTTransferEvent', or 'FTBurnEvent'; the event's 'asset_identifier' equals the predicate's 'asset_identifier'; AND the event's 'action' (derived from type) is in the predicate's 'actions' list.
Matching requires a strict equality check on 'asset_identifier'. If the event asset does not match the predicate asset, the event does not match, even if the action is accepted.

A transaction matches if it has events where the event type is 'NFTMintEvent', 'NFTTransferEvent', or 'NFTBurnEvent'; the event's 'asset_identifier' equals the predicate's 'asset_identifier'; AND the event's action is in the predicate's 'actions' list.
A transaction matches if it has events where the event type is 'STXMintEvent', 'STXTransferEvent', 'STXLockEvent', or 'STXBurnEvent'; AND the event's action is in the predicate's 'actions' list.
The predicate must NOT contain an 'asset_identifier' field. Asset filtering is not applicable.
The event's 'topic' must be 'print' for it to be considered for matching.
The event's 'contract_identifier' must exactly equal the predicate's 'contract_identifier' OR the predicate's 'contract_identifier' must be the wildcard '*'.
The decoded 'raw_value' of the event payload must contain the predicate's 'contains' substring (case-sensitive string search). OR the predicate's 'contains' must be the wildcard '*'.
The global 'triggered' flag is FALSE if no SmartContractEvent with topic 'print' matches the predicate across all blocks.
A transaction matches ONLY if its 'kind' is 'contract_deployment'. Furthermore, the deployed 'contract_identifier' of the transaction must START WITH the predicate's 'deployer' string.
No transactions are ever selected by this form of the predicate, regardless of the blocks analyzed. Result will always be triggered=false, count=0, empty list.

A transaction matches ONLY if its 'kind' is 'contract_call'. Additionally, its called contract identifier must equal the predicate's 'contract_identifier' AND its 'method' must equal the predicate's 'method'.
A transaction matches ONLY if its 'txid' exactly equals the predicate's 'equals' value. This applies regardless of the transaction's kind.
The field 'triggered' is set to 'true' if the count of selected blocks (apply_block_count) is > 0. Otherwise it is 'false'.

hide.replacement: apply strict equality checks on signature fields
